symbols: golden rays from the seraph to Francis's hands — the wounds of Christ imprinted on the saint's body
raised hands showing wounds — the stigmata, sharing in Christ's Passion
Franciscan gray habit with rope belt — poverty, humility, and obedience
seraph descending from golden clouds — the crucified Christ appearing in seraphic form
elaborate gilded frame — the sacred context of altarpiece devotion
description: A detail from an ornate gilded polyptych showing Saint Francis of Assisi in a gray Franciscan habit with a rope belt, standing with both hands raised, palms outward. His face is turned upward with an expression of ecstasy. From the upper left, a small winged figure (a seraph) descends from golden clouds, with golden rays or lines extending from it to Francis's hands and body. Francis has a tonsured head and a red halo. The background is a clear blue. The panel is surrounded by an extraordinarily ornate gilded frame with foliate and floral carved decorations.
